Problem Statement: In the following exercises, write the appropriate $$\varepsilon$$ - $$\delta$$ definition for each of the given statements.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = N$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, write the appropriate $$\varepsilon$$ - $$\delta$$ definition for each of the given statements.
Question: $$\underset{t \rightarrow b}{\text{lim}} g \left(\right. t \left.\right) = M$$
Answer: For every $$\varepsilon > 0 ,$$ there exists a $$\delta > 0 ,$$ so that if $$0 < | t - b | < \delta ,$$ then $$| g ( t ) - M | < \varepsilon$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, write the appropriate $$\varepsilon$$ - $$\delta$$ definition for each of the given statements.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow c}{\text{lim}} h \left(\right. x \left.\right) = L$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, write the appropriate $$\varepsilon$$ - $$\delta$$ definition for each of the given statements.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} \varphi \left(\right. x \left.\right) = A$$
Answer: For every $$\varepsilon > 0 ,$$ there exists a $$\delta > 0 ,$$ so that if $$0 < | x - a | < \delta ,$$ then $$| \phi ( x ) - A | < \varepsilon$$

Problem Statement: The following graph of the function f satisfies $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = 2 .$$ In the following exercises, determine a value of $$\delta > 0$$ that satisfies each statement.
Question: If $$0 < | x - 2 | < \delta ,$$ then $$| f ( x ) - 2 | < 1 .$$

Problem Statement: The following graph of the function f satisfies $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = 2 .$$ In the following exercises, determine a value of $$\delta > 0$$ that satisfies each statement.
Question: If $$0 < | x - 2 | < \delta ,$$ then $$| f ( x ) - 2 | < 0.5 .$$

Problem Statement: The following graph of the function f satisfies $$\underset{x \rightarrow 3}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = -1 .$$ In the following exercises, determine a value of $$\delta > 0$$ that satisfies each statement.
Question: If $$0 < | x - 3 | < \delta ,$$ then $$| f ( x ) + 1 | < 1 .$$

Problem Statement: The following graph of the function f satisfies $$\underset{x \rightarrow 3}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = -1 .$$ In the following exercises, determine a value of $$\delta > 0$$ that satisfies each statement.
Question: If $$0 < | x - 3 | < \delta ,$$ then $$| f ( x ) + 1 | < 2 .$$

Problem Statement: The following graph of the function f satisfies $$\underset{x \rightarrow 3}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = 2 .$$ In the following exercises, for each value of ε , find a value of $$\delta > 0$$ such that the precise definition of limit holds true.
Question: $$\varepsilon = 1.5$$

Problem Statement: The following graph of the function f satisfies $$\underset{x \rightarrow 3}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = 2 .$$ In the following exercises, for each value of ε , find a value of $$\delta > 0$$ such that the precise definition of limit holds true.
Question: $$\varepsilon = 3$$

Problem Statement: [T] In the following exercises, use a graphing calculator to find a number $$\delta$$ such that the statements hold true.
Question: $$| \text{sin} ( 2 x ) - \frac{1}{2} | < 0.1 ,$$ whenever $$| x - \frac{\pi}{12} | < \delta$$

Problem Statement: [T] In the following exercises, use a graphing calculator to find a number $$\delta$$ such that the statements hold true.
Question: $$| \sqrt{x - 4} - 2 | < 0.1 , \text{whenever} | x - 8 | < \delta$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. 5 x + 8 \left.\right) = 18$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 3}{\text{lim}} \frac{x^{2} - 9}{x - 3} = 6$$
Answer: Let $$\delta = \varepsilon .$$ If $$0 < | x - 3 | < \varepsilon ,$$ then $$| x + 3 - 6 | = | x - 3 | < \varepsilon .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} \frac{2 x^{2} - 3 x - 2}{x - 2} = 5$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} x^{4} = 0$$
Answer: Let $$\delta = \sqrt[4]{\varepsilon} .$$ If $$0 < | x | < \sqrt[4]{\varepsilon} ,$$ then $$| x^{4} | = x^{4} < \varepsilon .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. x^{2} + 2 x \left.\right) = 8$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given one-sided limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 5^{-}}{\text{lim}} \sqrt{5 - x} = 0$$
Answer: Let $$\delta = \varepsilon^{2} .$$ If $$5 - \varepsilon^{2} < x < 5 ,$$ then $$| \sqrt{5 - x} | = \sqrt{5 - x} < \varepsilon .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given one-sided limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0^{+}}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = -2 , \text{where} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \left{\right. 8 x - 3 , \text{if} x < 0 \\ 4 x - 2 , \text{if} x \geq 0 .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given one-sided limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 1^{-}}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = 3 , \text{where} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \left{\right. 5 x - 2 , \text{if} x < 1 \\ 7 x - 1 , \text{if} x \geq 1 .$$
Answer: Let $$\delta = \varepsilon \text{/} 5 .$$ If $$1 - \varepsilon \text{/} 5 < x < 1 ,$$ then $$| f ( x ) - 3 | = 5 x - 5 < \varepsilon .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} \frac{1}{x^{2}} = \infty$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow -1}{\text{lim}} \frac{3}{\left(\left(\right. x + 1 \left.\right)\right)^{2}} = \infty$$
Answer: Let $$\delta = \sqrt{\frac{3}{M}} .$$ If $$0 < | x + 1 | < \sqrt{\frac{3}{M}} ,$$ then $$f ( x ) = \frac{3}{( x + 1 )^{2}} > M .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 2}{\text{lim}} - \frac{1}{\left(\left(\right. x - 2 \left.\right)\right)^{2}} = - \infty$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: An engineer is using a machine to cut a flat square of Aerogel of area 144 cm 2 . If there is a maximum error tolerance in the area of 8 cm 2 , how accurately must the engineer cut on the side, assuming all sides have the same length? How do these numbers relate to $$\delta ,$$ ε , a , and L ?

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: Use the precise definition of limit to prove that the following limit does not exist: $$\underset{x \rightarrow 1}{\text{lim}} \frac{\left|\right. x - 1 \left|\right.}{x - 1} .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: Using precise definitions of limits, prove that $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ does not exist, given that $$f ( x )$$ is the ceiling function. ( Hint : Try any $$\delta < 1 \text{.)}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: Using precise definitions of limits, prove that $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ does not exist: $$f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \left{\right. 1 \text{if} x \text{is rational} \\ 0 \text{if} x \text{is irrational} .$$ ( Hint : Think about how you can always choose a rational number $$0 < r < d ,$$ but $$| f ( r ) - 0 | = 1 \text{.)}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: Using precise definitions of limits, determine $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ for $$f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \left{\right. x \text{if} x \text{is rational} \\ 0 \text{if} x \text{is irrational} .$$ ( Hint : Break into two cases, x rational and x irrational.)

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use the precise definition of limit to prove the given infinite limits.
Question: Using the function from the previous exercise, use the precise definition of limits to show that $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right)$$ does not exist for $$a \ne 0 .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, suppose that $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = L$$ and $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} g \left(\right. x \left.\right) = M$$ both exist. Use the precise definition of limits to prove the following limit laws:
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. f \left(\right. x \left.\right) + g \left(\right. x \left.\right) \left.\right) = L + M$$
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} a \right ) }{lim} ( f ( x ) ) + \underset{ \left ( x \vec{} a \right ) }{lim} ( g ( x ) ) \\ = L + M\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, suppose that $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = L$$ and $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} g \left(\right. x \left.\right) = M$$ both exist. Use the precise definition of limits to prove the following limit laws:
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} \left[\right. c f \left(\right. x \left.\right) \left]\right. = c L$$ for any real constant c ( Hint : Consider two cases: $$c = 0$$ and $$c \ne 0 \text{.)}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, suppose that $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = L$$ and $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} g \left(\right. x \left.\right) = M$$ both exist. Use the precise definition of limits to prove the following limit laws:
Question: $$\underset{x \rightarrow a}{\text{lim}} \left[\right. f \left(\right. x \left.\right) g \left(\right. x \left.\right) \left]\right. = L M .$$ ( Hint : $$| f ( x ) g ( x ) - L M | =$$ $$| f ( x ) g ( x ) - f ( x ) M + f ( x ) M - L M | \le | f ( x ) | | g ( x ) - M | + | M | | f ( x ) - L | \text{.)}$$
